author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
eo ta’ eka kalira celā
māthā neḍā, kapni parā,
tilaka nāke, galāya mālā

dekhte vaiṣṇavera mata,
āsala śākta kājera belā
sahaja⋅bhajana karchena māmu,
saṅge la’ye parera bālā

sakhībhāve bhajchena tā’re,
nije ha’ye nandalālā
kṛṣṇa⋅dāsera kathāra chale
mahājanake dicchena śalā

nava⋅rasika āpane māni’,
khācchena ābāra mana⋅kalā
bāula bale, dohāi o bhāi,
dūra kara e līlākhelā
en_translation:
This is indeed a disciple of Kali (the current age). Shaven-headed, wearing a kaupīna (loincloth), With tilaka on the nose, and mālā (beads) around the neck.

Looking like a Vaiṣṇava, But actually a Śākta (worshipper of the Divine Mother) in practice. The uncle is performing sahaja-bhajana (a pseudo-devotional practice), Taking another's woman along.

Worshipping her in the mood of a sakhī (female confidante of Rādhā), thinking himself to be Nandalāla. Under the pretext of Kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa's words, He is giving 'advice' (or subtly criticizing) the mahājana (great souls).

Considering himself a nava-rasika (a new "expert" in divine mellows), He is again indulging in 'mana-kolā' (self-deception/sense gratification). The Baul says, "I beg you, O brother, Abandon this līlā-khelā (pretense)."
